title: Soaring ammunition cost turns Iran conflict into 'war of stockpiles'
subhed: The daily interception of thousands of missiles and drones launched by Tehran over the past three weeks has put severe strain on already limited reserves. The situation is particularly critical for France, which is bound by defense agreements with several Middle Eastern countries.

title: UNRWA head Philippe Lazzarini's grim assessment: 'Without firm political and financial support, we face a slow death'
US and Israel attack Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, says Iran atomic energy organization
France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
subhed: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.

title: 'With Operation Epic Fury, the US turns pop culture into the language of Pentagon violence'
subhed: In an op-ed, Louis Lapeyrie, a researcher at Paris-I Panthéon-Sorbonne University, examines the visual and narrative shifts in military communications under the Trump administration and explores the many borrowings from video games.

title: An ultra-modern artist: 'Renoir and Love' and 'Renoir Drawings' at the Musée d'Orsay
subhed: Challenging his reputation as a sentimental artist, two exhibitions of paintings and drawings highlight Renoir's affinity for working-class subjects and the complexity of his compositions.

title: Despite exhaustion and fear of Iranian strikes, Israelis continue to support warring government
subhed: Nearly 80% of the population continues to support Benjamin Netanyahu's government after three weeks of conflict with Iran, fueled by the authorities' repeated announcements of victory.

subhed: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 1/4. Le Monde asked writers Richard Ford, Zeruya Shalev and Navid Kermani for their perspectives on the war.

title: Bertrand Badie, political scientist: 'Persian culture is an impetuous alliance of dignity and fury'
subhed: The international relations specialist, who is of Iranian descent on his father's side, speaks with Le Monde about the West's lack of understanding of Iran and its culture.

title: Anne-Claire Legendre, new president of France's Arab World Institute: 'Our role is to provide tools for understanding'
subhed: Legendre, who replaced former culture minister Jack Lang in February, speaks to Le Monde about her priorities for revitalizing the institution.

title: War in Iran: 'Europe said no to the US. This is new, and it is a major development'
